The heavyweight boxer is famous for his incredible power punches.
His aggressive fighting style often overwhelms his opponents in the ring.
Fans have been waiting for these highly anticipated matches for years.
In their very first match, the boxer landed exactly 120 power punches.
This initial number set a high bar for the rest of the series.
During the second match, he decided to increase his output significantly.
He managed to land twenty-five percent more power punches than before.
To find the new total, we must calculate twenty-five percent of 120.
Twenty-five percent of 120 is equal to 30 additional power punches.
Adding these 30 punches to the original 120 gives us the second match total.
Therefore, he landed 150 power punches in that second fight.
The crowd was absolutely electrified by this impressive performance.
Moving on to the third match, the boxer continued his dominant streak.
He managed to land 30 more power punches than he did in the second match.
Since the second match total was 150, we add 30 to that number.
This calculation results in a total of 180 power punches for the third fight.
It is remarkable how he consistently improved his numbers each time.
Now we must sum up the punches from all three separate matches.
The first match contributed 120 punches to the grand total.
The second match added another 150 punches to the count.
The third match brought an additional 180 punches to the sum.
Adding 120 and 150 together gives us a subtotal of 270 punches.
When we add the final 180 punches to that 270, we get the result.
The final sum of all power punches across the three matches is 450.
This total demonstrates the sheer dominance of the heavyweight fighter.
His ability to land so many heavy blows is truly a testament to his skill.
The series of matches will likely be remembered as a classic in boxing history.
Such a high number of power punches is rare in professional boxing.
The opponent must have been exhausted by the relentless assault.
Calculating the total requires careful attention to each individual match.
Every single punch counted towards this massive final number.
